description:
You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who has been asked to enforce a “CPU-limit” policy across a (simulated) three-node Kubernetes cluster.  
    A snapshot of the cluster’s kubelet logs has already been collected for you.

    1.  LOG LOCATION  
        • All logs live in /home/user/cluster_logs/  
            – node-alpha.log  
            – node-bravo.log  
            – node-charlie.log  

    2.  WHAT THE LOGS LOOK LIKE  
        Every line follows the exact pattern shown below (one line = one pod start event):  
            YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ kubelet[PID]: Started pod: <pod-name> (cpu_limit=<value>)  
        The literal string **cpu_limit=none** means the pod started without any CPU limit defined.

    3.  REQUIRED ANALYSIS  
        • Parse every *.log file in /home/user/cluster_logs/.  
        • Count how many pods were started in total.  
        • Identify all pods that have cpu_limit=none.  
        • Produce a single JSON-formatted compliance report at  
            /home/user/reports/cpu_compliance_report.json

        EXACT JSON SCHEMA (all keys required, no extras):
        {
          "checked_at": "2024-06-06T10:00:00Z",
          "total_pods": <integer>,
          "non_compliant": <integer>,
          "details": [
            {
              "pod": "<pod-name>",
              "node": "<node-name>",
              "reason": "missing cpu limit"
            }
            …                        <-- zero or more identical objects
          ]
        }

        • The objects inside "details" MUST be sorted alphabetically by the pod field.

    4.  POLICY REMEDIATION  
        • Open the file /home/user/policies/secured-deployment.yaml.  
          At present it contains a single container block that has **no resources section**.  
        • Add a resources → limits → cpu stanza so that the container ends up looking **exactly** like this (spacing is 2-space indent):
              - name: insecure-container
                image: nginx:latest
                resources:
                  limits:
                    cpu: "500m"

    5.  MITIGATION LOG  
        • Append (or create if absent) a plain-text file  
            /home/user/reports/mitigation.log  
        • Add ONE new line representing what you just did.  
        • Use the fixed timestamp “2024-06-06 10:00:00” followed by a pipe (“|”) and the message shown below:
            2024-06-06 10:00:00|Updated /home/user/policies/secured-deployment.yaml - inserted cpu limit enforcement

    6.  WHAT WILL BE AUTOMATICALLY TESTED  
        a. /home/user/reports/cpu_compliance_report.json exists and is valid JSON.  
        b. checked_at = "2024-06-06T10:00:00Z".  
        c. total_pods equals the number of log lines across the three files.  
        d. non_compliant equals the size of the details array.  
        e. Every object in details has the required 3 keys with correct values sorted by pod.  
        f. /home/user/policies/secured-deployment.yaml now contains the cpu limit stanza (exact text).  
        g. /home/user/reports/mitigation.log contains the single line shown in step 5.

    Accomplish everything directly from the Linux terminal.  
    IMPORTANT: Do not display the contents of any secret test data; simply perform the steps so that the files match the specification above.
truth:
<!--  Pre-existing files that are already on disk before the agent starts  -->
    <!--  The test harness will create them exactly as declared here.          -->

    <!-- Directory structure -->
    /home/user/cluster_logs/
    /home/user/policies/
    /home/user/reports/

    <!-- Log files -->
    /home/user/cluster_logs/node-alpha.log  (content)
    2024-06-06T08:15:42Z kubelet[987]: Started pod: auth-5d9c8d7cdf-abcde (cpu_limit=250m)
    2024-06-06T08:16:10Z kubelet[987]: Started pod: billing-6c8dcbf9f-xyz12 (cpu_limit=none)
    2024-06-06T08:16:35Z kubelet[987]: Started pod: payments-7f9d7c9d7f-q2g7x (cpu_limit=250m)

    /home/user/cluster_logs/node-bravo.log  (content)
    2024-06-06T08:20:11Z kubelet[123]: Started pod: cart-7c9d7c9d7f-mnopq (cpu_limit=none)
    2024-06-06T08:21:11Z kubelet[123]: Started pod: search-6c8dcbf9f-rstuv (cpu_limit=250m)

    /home/user/cluster_logs/node-charlie.log  (content)
    2024-06-06T08:25:07Z kubelet[456]: Started pod: catalogue-5d9c8d7cdf-wxyz1 (cpu_limit=250m)

    <!-- Policy file with no resources block -->
    /home/user/policies/secured-deployment.yaml  (content)
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: insecure-service
    spec:
      replicas: 2
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: insecure-service
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: insecure-container
            image: nginx:latest

    <!-- Empty reports directory initially -->
    (no files yet in /home/user/reports/)

    ------------------------------------------------------------------
    EXPECTED STATE AFTER SUCCESSFUL AGENT EXECUTION
    ------------------------------------------------------------------

    1. /home/user/reports/cpu_compliance_report.json  (content)
    {
      "checked_at": "2024-06-06T10:00:00Z",
      "total_pods": 6,
      "non_compliant": 2,
      "details": [
        {
          "pod": "billing-6c8dcbf9f-xyz12",
          "node": "node-alpha",
          "reason": "missing cpu limit"
        },
        {
          "pod": "cart-7c9d7c9d7f-mnopq",
          "node": "node-bravo",
          "reason": "missing cpu limit"
        }
      ]
    }

    2. /home/user/policies/secured-deployment.yaml now contains:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: insecure-service
    spec:
      replicas: 2
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: insecure-service
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: insecure-container
            image: nginx:latest
            resources:
              limits:
                cpu: "500m"

    3. /home/user/reports/mitigation.log  (content)
    2024-06-06 10:00:00|Updated /home/user/policies/secured-deployment.yaml - inserted cpu limit enforcement
